source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the fourth generation they shall return hither: for as yet the iniquities of the Amorrhites are not at the full until this present time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the sun was set, there arose a dark mist, and there appeared a smoking furnace, and a lamp of fire passing between those divisions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That day God made a covenant with Abram, saying: To thy seed will I give this land, from the river to Egypt even to the great river Euphrates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Cineans, and Cenezites, the Cedmonites,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Hethites, and the Pherezites, the Raphaim also,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Amorrhites, and the Chanaanites, and the Gergesites, and the Jebusites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Sarai, the wife of Abram, had brought forth no children: but having a handmaid, an Egyptian, named Agar,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: She said to her husband: Behold, the Lord hath restrained me from bearing: go in unto my handmaid, it may be I may have children of her at least. And when he agreed to her request,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: She took Agar the Egyptian her handmaid, ten years after they first dwelt in the land of Chanaan, and gave her to her husband to wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he went in to her. But she perceiving that she was with child, despised her mistress.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sarai said to Abram: Thou dost unjustly with me: I gave my handmaid into thy bosom, and she perceiving herself to be with child, despiseth me. The Lord judge between me and thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abram made answer, and said to her: Behold thy handmaid is in thy own hand, use her as it pleaseth thee. And when Sarai afflicted her, she ran away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel of the Lord having found her, by a fountain of water in the wilderness, which is in the way to Sur in the desert,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to her: Agar, handmaid of Sarai, whence comest thou? and whither goest thou? And she answered: I flee from the face of Sarai, my mistress.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel of the Lord said to her: Return to thy mistress, and humble thyself under her hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again he said: I will multiply thy seed exceedingly, and it shall not be numbered for multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again: Behold, said he, thou art with child, and thou shalt bring forth a son: and thou shalt call his name Ismael, because the Lord hath heard thy affliction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall be a wild man: his hand will be against all men, and all men’s hands against him: and he shall pitch his tents over against all his brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she called the name of the Lord that spoke unto her: Thou the God who hast seen me. For she said: Verily, here have I seen the hinder parts of him that seeth me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore she called that well, the well of him that liveth and seeth me. The same is between Cades and Barad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Agar brought forth a son to Abram: who called his name Ismael.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abram was four score and six years old when Agar brought him forth Ismael.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after he began to be ninety and nine years old, the Lord appeared to him: and said unto him: I am the Almighty God: walk before me, and be perfect.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make my covenant between me and thee: and I will multiply thee exceedingly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abram fell flat on his face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God said to him: I am, and my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shall thy name be called any more Abram: but thou shalt be called Abraham: because I have made thee a father of many nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make thee increase exceedingly, and I will make nations of thee, and kings shall come out of thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee, and between thy seed after thee in their generations, by a perpetual covenant: to be a God to thee, and to thy seed after thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will give to thee, and to thy seed, the land of thy sojournment, all the land of Chanaan, for a perpetual possession, and I will be their God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again God said to Abraham: And thou therefore shalt keep my covenant, and thy seed after thee in their generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is my covenant which you shall observe between me and you, and thy seed after thee: All the male-kind of you shall be circumcised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin, that it may be for a sign of the covenant between me and you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An infant of eight days old shall be circumcised among you, every manchild in your generations: he that is born in the house, as well as the bought servant, shall be circumcised, and whosoever is not of your stock:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my covenant shall be in your flesh for a perpetual covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The male whose flesh of his foreskin shall not be circumcised, that soul shall be destroyed out of his people: because he hath broken my covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God said also to Abraham: Sarai thy wife thou shalt not call Sarai, but Sara.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will bless her, and of her I will give thee a son, whom I will bless, and he shall become nations, and kings of people shall spring from him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, saying in his heart: Shall a son, thinkest thou, be born to him that is a hundred years old? and shall Sara that is ninety years old bring forth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to God: O that Ismael may live before thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God said to Abraham: Sara thy wife shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name Isaac, and I will establish my covenant with him for a perpetual covenant, and with his seed after him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as for Ismael I have also heard thee. Behold, I will bless him, and increase, and multiply him exceedingly: he shall beget twelve chiefs, and I will make him a great nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sara shall bring forth to thee at this time in the next year.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had left off speaking with him, God went up from Abraham.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham took Ismael his son, and all that were born in his house: and all whom he had bought, every male among the men of his house: and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskin forthwith the very same day, as God had commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham was ninety and nine years old, when he circumcised the flesh of his foreskin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ismael his son was full thirteen years old at the time of his circumcision.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The self-same day was Abraham circumcised and Ismael his son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the men of his house, as well they that were born in his house, as the bought servants and strangers, were circumcised with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord appeared to him in the vale of Mambre as he was sitting at the door of his tent, in the very heat of the day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had lifted up his eyes, there appeared to him three men standing near to him: and as soon as he saw them, he ran to meet them from the door of his tent, and adored down to the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Lord, if I have found favour in thy sight, pass not away from thy servant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I will fetch a little water, and wash ye your feet, and rest ye under the tree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will set a morsel of bread, and strengthen ye your heart, afterwards you shall pass on: for therefore are you come aside to your servant. And they said: Do as thou hast spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham made haste into the tent to Sara, and said to her: Make haste, temper together three measures of flour, and make cakes upon the hearth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he himself ran to the herd, and took from thence a calf, very tender and very good, and gave it to a young man, who made haste and boiled it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He took also butter and milk, and the calf which he had boiled, and set before them: but he stood by them under the tree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they had eaten, they said to him: Where is Sara thy wife? He answered: Lo she is in the tent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to him: I will return and come to thee at this time, life accompanying, and Sara, thy wife, shall have a son. Which when Sara heard, she laughed behind the door of the tent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now they were both old, and far advanced in years, and it had ceased to be with Sara after the manner of women.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she laughed secretly, saying: After I am grown old, and my lord is an old man, shall I give myself to pleasure?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Abraham: Why did Sara laugh, saying: Shall I, who am an old woman, bear a child indeed?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is there any thing hard to God? According to appointment I will return to thee at this same time, life accompanying, and Sara shall have a son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sara denied, saying: I did not laugh: for she was afraid. But the Lord said: Nay; but thou didst laugh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the men rose up from thence, they turned their eyes towards Sodom: and Abraham walked with them, bringing them on the way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said: Can I hide from Abraham what I am about to do:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seeing he shall become a great and mighty nation, and in him all the nations of the earth shall be blessed?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I know that he will command his children, and his household after him, to keep the way of the Lord, and do judgment and justice: that for Abraham’s sake, the Lord may bring to effect all the things he hath spoken unto him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said: The cry of Sodom and Gomorrha is multiplied, and their sin is become exceedingly grievous.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will go down and see whether they have done according to the cry that is come to me; or whether it be not so, that I may know.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they turned themselves from thence, and went their way to Sodom: but Abraham as yet stood before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And drawing nigh, he said: Wilt thou destroy the just with the wicked?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If there be fifty just men in the city, shall they perish withal? and wilt thou not spare that place for the sake of the fifty just, if they be therein?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Far be it from thee to do this thing, and to slay the just with the wicked, and for the just to be in like case as the wicked; this is not beseeming thee: thou who judgest all the earth, wilt not make this judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to him: If I find in Sodom fifty just within the city, I will spare the whole place for their sake.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham answered, and said: Seeing I have once begun, I will speak to my Lord, whereas I am dust and ashes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What if there be five less than fifty just persons? wilt thou for five and forty destroy the whole city: And he said: I will not destroy it, if I find five and forty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again he said to him: But if forty be found there, what wilt thou do? He said: I will not destroy it for the sake of forty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lord, saith he, be not angry, I beseech thee, if I speak: What if thirty shall be found there? He answered: I will not do it, if I find thirty there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seeing, saith he, I have once begun, I will speak to my Lord: What if twenty be found there? He said: I will not destroy it for the sake of twenty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I beseech thee, saith he, be not angry, Lord, if I speak yet once more: What if ten shall be found there? And he said: I will not destroy it for the sake of ten.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord departed, after he had left speaking to Abraham: and Abraham returned to his place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the two angels came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gate of the city. And seeing them, he rose up and went to meet them: and worshipped prostrate to the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said: I beseech you, my lords, turn in to the house of your servant, and lodge there: wash your feet, and in the morning you shall go on your way. And they said: No, but we will abide in the street.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He pressed them very much to turn in unto him: and when they were come into his house, he made them a feast, and baked unleavened bread, and they ate:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But before they went to bed, the men of the city beset the house, both young and old, all the people together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they called Lot, and said to him: Where are the men that came in to thee at night? bring them out hither, that we may know them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lot went out to them, and shut the door after him, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do not so, I beseech you, my brethren, do not commit this evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have two daughters who, as yet, have not known man; I will bring them out to you, and abuse you them as it shall please you, so that you do no evil to these men, because they are come in under the shadow of my roof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they said: Get thee back thither. And again: Thou camest in, said they, as a stranger, was it to be a judge? therefore we will afflict thee more than them. And they pressed very violently upon Lot: and they were even at the point of breaking open the doors.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold the men put out their hand, and drew in Lot unto them, and shut the door.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And them, that were without, they struck with blindness from the least to the greatest, so that they could not find the door.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they said to Lot: Hast thou here any of thine? son in law, or sons, or daughters, all that are thine bring them out of this city:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For we will destroy this place, because their cry is grown loud before the Lord, who hath sent us to destroy them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Lot went out, and spoke to his sons in law that were to have his daughters, and said: Arise: get you out of this place, because the Lord will destroy this city. And he seemed to them to speak as it were in jest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when it was morning, the angels pressed him, saying: Arise, take thy wife, and the two daughters that thou hast: lest thou also perish in the wickedness of the city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as he lingered, they took his hand, and the hand of his wife, and of his two daughters, because the Lord spared him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they brought him forth, and set him without the city: and there they spoke to him, saying: Save thy life: look not back, neither stay thou in all the country about: but save thy self in the mountain, lest thou be also consumed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Lot said to them: I beseech thee, my Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thy servant hath found grace before thee, and thou hast magnified thy mercy, which thou hast shewn to me, in saving my life, and I cannot escape to the mountain, lest some evil seize me, and I die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is this city here at hand, to which I may flee, it is a little one, and I shall be saved in it: is it not a little one, and my soul shall live?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to him: Behold also in this, I have heard thy prayers, not to destroy the city for which thou hast spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Make haste, and be saved there: because I cannot do any thing till thou go in thither. Therefore the name of that city was called Segor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sun was risen upon the earth, and Lot entered into Segor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord rained upon Sodom and Gomorrha brimstone and fire from the Lord out of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he destroyed these cities, and all the country about, all the inhabitants of the cities, and all things that spring from the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his wife looking behind her, was turned into a statue of salt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham got up early in the morning, and in the place where he had stood before with the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He looked towards Sodom and Gomorrha, and the whole land of that country: and he saw the ashes rise up from the earth as the smoke of a furnace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now when God destroyed the cities of that country, remembering Abraham, he delivered Lot out of the destruction of the cities wherein he had dwelt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Lot went up out of Segor, and abode in the mountain, and his two daughters with him (for he was afraid to stay in Segor) and he dwelt in a cave, he and his two daughters with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the elder said to the younger: Our father is old, and there is no man left on the earth, to come in unto us after the manner of the whole earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come, let us make him drunk with wine, and let us lie with him, that we may preserve seed of our father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they made their father drink wine that night: and the elder went in, and lay with her father: but he perceived not, neither when his daughter lay down, nor when she rose up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the next day the elder said to the younger: Behold I lay last night with my father, let us make him drink wine also to night, and thou shalt lie with him, that we may save seed of our father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They made their father drink wine that night also, and the younger daughter went in, and lay with him: and neither then did he perceive when she lay down, nor when she rose up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So the two daughters of Lot were with child by their father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the elder bore a son, and she called his name Moab: he is the father of the Moabites unto this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The younger also bore a son, and she called his name Ammon; that is, the son of my people: he is the father of the Ammonites unto this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham removed from thence to the south country, and dwelt between Cades and Sur, and sojourned in Gerara.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said of Sara his wife: She is my sister. So Abimelech the king of Gerara sent, and took her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and he said to him: Lo thou shalt die for the woman that thou hast taken: for she hath a husband.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Abimelech had not touched her, and he said: Lord, wilt thou slay a nation that is ignorant and just?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Did not he say to me: She is my sister: and she say, He is my brother? in the simplicity of my heart, and cleanness of my hands have I done this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God said to him: And I know that thou didst it with a sincere heart: and therefore I withheld thee from sinning against me, and I suffered thee not to touch her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now therefore restore the man his wife, for he is a prophet: and he shall pray for thee, and thou shalt live: but if thou wilt not restore her, know that thou shalt surely die, thou and all that are thine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abimelech forthwith rising up in the night, called all his servants: and spoke all these words in their hearing, and all the men were exceedingly afraid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abimelech called also for Abraham, and said to him: What hast thou done to us? what have we offended thee in, that thou hast brought upon me and upon my kingdom a great sin? thou hast done to us what thou oughtest not to do.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again he expostulated with him, and said: What sawest thou, that thou hast done this?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham answered: I thought with myself, saying: Perhaps there is not the fear of God in this place: and they will kill me for the sake of my wife:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Howbeit, otherwise also she is truly my sister, the daughter of my father, and not the daughter of my mother, and I took her to wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after God brought me out of my father’s house, I said to her: Thou shalt do me this kindness: In every place, to which we shall come, thou shalt say that I am thy brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and servants and handmaids, and gave to Abraham: and restored to him Sara his wife,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said: The land is before you, dwell wheresoever it shall please thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to Sara he said: Behold I have given thy brother a thousand pieces of silver, this shall serve thee for a covering of thy eyes to all that are with thee, and whithersoever thou shalt go: and remember thou wast taken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Abraham prayed, God healed Abimelech and his wife, and his handmaids, and they bore children:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord had closed up every womb of the house of Abimelech, on account of Sara, Abraham’s wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord visited Sara, as he had promised: and fulfilled what he had spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she conceived and bore a son in her old age, at the time that God had foretold her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham called the name of his son, whom Sara bore him, Isaac.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he circumcised him the eighth day, as God had commanded him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When he was a hundred years old: for at this age of his father, was Isaac born.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sara said: God hath made a laughter for me: whosoever shall hear of it will laugh with me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again she said: Who would believe that Abraham should hear that Sara gave suck to a son, whom she bore to him in his old age?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the child grew, and was weaned: and Abraham made a great feast on the day of his weaning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Sara had seen the son of Agar, the Egyptian, playing with Isaac, her son, she said to Abraham:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cast out this bondwoman and her son; for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham took this grievously for his son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God said to him: Let it not seem grievous to thee for the boy, and for thy bondwoman: in all that Sara hath said to thee, hearken to her voice: for in Isaac shall thy seed be called.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I will make the son also of the bondwoman a great nation, because he is thy seed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Abraham rose up in the morning, and taking bread and a bottle of water, put it upon her shoulder, and delivered the boy, and sent her away. And she departed, and wandered in the wilderness of Bersabee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the water in the bottle was spent, she cast the boy under one of the trees that were there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she went her way, and sat over against him a great way off, as far as a bow can carry, for she said: I will not see the boy die: and sitting over against, she lifted up her voice and wept.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God heard the voice of the boy: and an angel of God called to Agar from heaven, saying: What art thou doing, Agar? fear not; for God hath heard the voice of the boy, from the place wherein he is.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise, take up the boy, and hold him by the hand, for I will make him a great nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God opened her eyes: and she saw a well of water, and went and filled the bottle, and gave the boy to drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God was with him: and he grew, and dwelt in the wilderness, and became a young man, an archer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Pharan, and his mother took a wife for him out of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the same time Abimelech, and Phicol the general of his army, said to Abraham: God is with thee in all that thou dost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Swear therefore by God, that thou wilt not hurt me, nor my posterity, nor my stock: but according to the kindness that I have done to thee, thou shalt do to me, and to the land wherein thou hast lived a stranger.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham said: I will swear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he reproved Abimelech for a well of water, which his servants had taken away by force.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abimelech answered: I knew not who did this thing: and thou didst not tell me, and I heard not of it till today.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Abraham took sheep and oxen, and gave them to Abimelech: and both of them made a league.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham set apart seven ewelambs of the flock.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abimelech said to him: What mean these seven ewelambs which thou hast set apart?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he said: Thou shalt take seven ewelambs at my hand: that they may be a testimony for me, that I dug this well.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore that place was called Bersabee; because there both of them did swear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they made a league for the well of oath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abimelech and Phicol, the general of his army, arose and returned to the land of the Palestines. But Abraham planted a grove in Bersabee, and there called upon the name of the Lord God eternal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was a sojourner in the land of the Palestines many days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After these things, God tempted Abraham, and said to him: Abraham, Abraham. And he answered: Here I am.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to him: Take thy only begotten son Isaac, whom thou lovest, and go into the land of vision; and there thou shalt offer him for an holocaust upon one of the mountains which I will shew thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Abraham rising up in the night, saddled his ass, and took with him two young men, and Isaac his son: and when he had cut wood for the holocaust, he went his way to the place which God had commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the third day, lifting up his eyes, he saw the place afar off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to his young men: Stay you here with the ass; I and the boy will go with speed as far as yonder, and after we have worshipped, will return to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he took the wood for the holocaust, and laid it upon Isaac his son; and he himself carried in his hands fire and a sword. And as they two went on together,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Isaac said to his father: My father. And he answered: What wilt thou, son? Behold, saith he, fire and wood: where is the victim for the holocaust?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham said: God will provide himself a victim for an holocaust, my son. So they went on together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they came to the place which God had shewn him, where he built an altar, and laid the wood in order upon it; and when he had bound Isaac his son, he laid him on the altar upon the pile of wood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he put forth his hand, and took the sword, to sacrifice his son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold, an angel of the Lord from heaven called to him, saying: Abraham, Abraham. And he answered: Here I am.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to him: Lay not thy hand upon the boy, neither do thou any thing to him: now I know that thou fearest God, and hast not spared thy only begotten son for my sake.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw behind his back a ram, amongst the briers, sticking fast by the horns, which he took and offered for a holocaust instead of his son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he called the name of that place, The Lord seeth. Whereupon, even to this day, it is said: In the mountain the Lord will see.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel of the Lord called to Abraham a second time from heaven, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By my own self have I sworn, saith the Lord: because thou hast done this thing, and hast not spared thy only begotten son for my sake:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will bless thee, and I will multiply thy seed as the stars of heaven, and as the sand that is by the sea shore; thy seed shall possess the gates of their enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed, because thou hast obeyed my voice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham returned to his young men, and they went to Bersabee together, and he dwelt there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After these things, it was told Abraham, that Melcha also had borne children to Nachor his brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hus, the firstborn, and Buz, his brother, and Camuel the father of the Syrians,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Cased, and Azau, and Pheldas, and Jedlaph,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Bathuel, of whom was born Rebecca: these eight did Melcha bear to Nachor, Abraham’s brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his concubine, named Roma, bore Tabee, and Gaham, and Tahas, and Maacha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sara lived a hundred and twenty-seven years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she died in the city of Arbee which is Hebron, in the land of Chanaan: and Abraham came to mourn and weep for her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after he rose up from the funeral obsequies, he spoke to the children of Heth, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am a stranger and sojourner among you: give me the right of a burying place with you, that I may bury my dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The children of Heth answered, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My lord, hear us, thou art a prince of God among us: bury thy dead in our principal sepulchres: and no man shall have power to hinder thee from burying thy dead in his sepulchre.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham rose up, and bowed down to the people of the land, to wit, the children of Heth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to them: If it please your soul that I should bury my dead, hear me, and intercede for me to Ephron the son of Seor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That he may give me the double cave, which he hath in the end of his field: For as much money as it is worth he shall give it me before you, for a possession of a burying place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Ephron dwelt in the midst of the children of Heth. And Ephron made answer to Abraham in the hearing of all that went in at the gate of the city, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let it not be so, my lord, but do thou rather hearken to what I say: The field I deliver to thee, and the cave that is therein; in the presence of the children of my people, bury thy dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham bowed down before the people of the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he spoke to Ephron, in the presence of the people: I beseech thee to hear me: I will give money for the field; take it, and so will I bury my dead in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ephron answered:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My lord, hear me. The ground which thou desirest, is worth four hundred sicles of silver: this is the price between me and thee: but what is this? bury thy dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Abraham had heard this, he weighed out the money that Ephron had asked, in the hearing of the children of Heth, four hundred sicles of silver, of common current money.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the field that before was Ephron’s, wherein was the double cave, looking towards Mambre, both it and the cave, and all the trees thereof, in all its limits round about,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Was made sure to Abraham for a possession, in the sight of the children of Heth, and of all that went in at the gate of his city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And so Abraham buried Sara, his wife, in the double cave of the field, that looked towards Mambre, this is Hebron in the land of Chanaan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the field was made sure to Abraham, and the cave that was in it, for a possession to bury in, by the children of Heth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Abraham was old, and advanced in age; and the Lord had blessed him in all things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to the elder servant of his house, who was ruler over all he had: Put thy hand under my thigh,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That I may make thee swear by the Lord, the God of heaven and earth, that thou take not a wife for my son, of the daughters of the Chanaanites, among whom I dwell:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But that thou go to my own country and kindred, and take a wife from thence for my son Isaac.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The servant answered: If the woman will not come with me into this land, must I bring thy son back again to the place from whence thou camest out?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham said: Beware thou never bring my son back again thither.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord God of heaven, who took me out of my father’s house, and out of my native country, who spoke to me, and swore to me, saying: To thy seed will I give this land: he will send his angel before thee, and thou shalt take from thence a wife for my son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if the woman will not follow thee, thou shalt not be bound by the oath: only bring not my son back thither again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The servant, therefore, put his hand under the thigh of Abraham, his lord, and swore to him upon his word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he took ten camels of his master’s herd, and departed, carrying something of all his goods with him, and he set forward and went on to Mesopotamia, to the city of Nachor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had made the camels lie down without the town, near a well of water, in the evening, at the time when women are wont to come out to draw water, he said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, the God of my master, Abraham, meet me today, I beseech thee, and shew kindness to my master, Abraham.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I stand nigh the spring of water, and the daughters of the inhabitants of this city will come out to draw water:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now, therefore, the maid to whom I shall say: Let down thy pitcher that I may drink: and she shall answer, Drink, and I will give thy camels drink also: let it be the same whom thou hast provided for thy servant Isaac: and by this, I shall understand that thou hast shewn kindness to my master.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He had not yet ended these words within himself, and behold Rebecca came out, the daughter of Bathuel, son of Melcha, wife to Nachor the brother of Abraham, having a pitcher on her shoulder:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An exceeding comely maid, and a most beautiful virgin, and not known to man: and she went down to the spring, and filled her pitcher, and was coming back.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the servant ran to meet her, and said: Give me a little water to drink of thy pitcher.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she answered: Drink, my lord. And quickly she let down the pitcher upon her arm, and gave him drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had drunk, she said: I will draw water for thy camels also, till they all drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And pouring out the pitcher into the troughs, she ran back to the well to draw water; and having drawn, she gave to all the camels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he musing, beheld her with silence, desirous to know whether the Lord had made his journey prosperous or not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after that the camels had drunk, the man took out golden earrings, weighing two sicles; and as many bracelets, of ten sicles weight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to her: Whose daughter art thou? tell me: is there any place in thy father’s house to lodge?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she answered: I am the daughter of Bathuel, the son of Melcha, whom she bore to Nachor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she said, moreover, to him: We have good store of both straw and hay, and a large place to lodge in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The man bowed himself down, and adored the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Blessed be the Lord God of my master Abraham, who hath not taken away his mercy and truth from my master, and hath brought me the straight way into the house of my master’s brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the maid ran, and told in her mother’s house all that she had heard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Rebecca had a brother, named Laban, who went out in haste to the man, to the well.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had seen the earrings and bracelets in his sister’s hands, and had heard all that she related, saying, Thus and thus the man spoke to me: he came to the man who stood by the camels, and near to the spring of water,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to him: Come in, thou blessed of the Lord; why standest thou without? I have prepared the house, and a place for the camels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he brought him into his lodging; and he unharnessed the camels, and gave straw and hay, and water to wash his feet, and the feet of the men that were come with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And bread was set before him. But he said: I will not eat, till I tell my message. He answered him: Speak.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: I am the servant of Abraham:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord hath blessed my master wonderfully, and he is become great: and he hath given him sheep and oxen, silver and gold, men servants and women servants, camels and asses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sara, my master’s wife, hath borne my master a son in her old age, and he hath given him all that he had.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my master made me swear, saying: Thou shalt not take a wife for my son of the Chanaanites, in whose land I dwell:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt go to my father’s house, and shalt take a wife of my own kindred for my son:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I answered my master: What if the woman will not come with me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord, said he, in whose sight I walk, will send his angel with thee, and will direct thy way: and thou shalt take a wife for my son of my own kindred, and of my father’s house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt be clear from my curse, when thou shalt come to my kindred, if they will not give thee one.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I came today to the well of water, and said: O Lord God of my master, Abraham, if thou hast prospered my way, wherein I now walk,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I stand by the well of water, and the virgin, that shall come out to draw water, who shall hear me say: Give me a little water to drink of thy pitcher:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall say to me: Both drink thou, and I will also draw for thy camels: let the same be the woman, whom the Lord hath prepared for my master’s son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whilst I pondered these things secretly with myself, Rebecca appeared, coming with a pitcher, which she carried on her shoulder: and she went down to the well and drew water. And I said to her: Give me a little to drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she speedily let down the pitcher from her shoulder, and said to me: Both drink thou, and to thy camels I will give drink. I drank, and she watered the camels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I asked her, and said: Whose daughter art thou? And she answered: I am the daughter of Bathuel, the son of Nachor, whom Melcha bore to him. So I put earrings on her to adorn her face, and I put bracelets on her hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And falling down, I adored the Lord, blessing the Lord God of my master, Abraham, who hath brought me the straight way to take the daughter of my master’s brother for his son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore, if you do according to mercy and truth with my master, tell me: but if it please you otherwise, tell me that also, that I may go to the right hand, or to the left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Laban and Bathuel answered: The word hath proceeded from the Lord: we cannot speak any other thing to thee but his pleasure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, Rebecca is before thee, take her and go thy way, and let her be the wife of thy master’s son, as the Lord hath spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which when Abraham’s servant heard, falling down to the ground, he adored the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And bringing forth vessels of silver and gold, and garments, he gave them to Rebecca, for a present. He offered gifts also to her brothers, and to her mother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a banquet was made, and they ate and drank together, and lodged there. And in the morning, the servant arose, and said: Let me depart, that I may go to my master.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And her brother and mother answered: Let the maid stay, at least, ten days with us, and afterwards she shall depart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Stay me not, said he, because the Lord hath prospered my way: send me away, that I may go to my master.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they said: Let us call the maid, and ask her will.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they called her, and when she was come, they asked: Wilt thou go with this man? She said: I will go.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they sent her away, and her nurse, and Abraham’s servant, and his company.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wishing prosperity to their sister, and saying: Thou art our sister, mayst thou increase to thousands of thousands; and may thy seed possess the gates of their enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Rebecca and her maids, being set upon camels, followed the man: who with speed returned to his master.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the same time, Isaac was walking along the way to the well which is called Of the living and the seeing: for he dwelt in the south country:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was gone forth to meditate in the field, the day being now well spent: and when he had lifted up his eyes, he saw camels coming afar off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rebecca also, when she saw Isaac, lighted off the camel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to the servant: Who is that man who cometh towards us along the field? And he said to her: That man is my master. But she quickly took her cloak, and covered herself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the servant told Isaac all that he had done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who brought her into the tent of Sara his mother, and took her to wife: and he loved her so much, that it moderated the sorrow which was occasioned by his mother’s death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham married another wife named Cetura:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who bore him Zamram, and Jecsan, and Madan, and Madian, and Jesboc, and Sue.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jecsan also begot Saba, and Dadan. The children of Dadan were Assurim, and Latusim, and Loomim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But of Madian was born Epha, and Opher, and Henoch, and Abida, and Eldaa: all these were the children of Cetura.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abraham gave all his possessions to Isaac:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the children of the concubines he gave gifts, and separated them from Isaac his son, while he yet lived, to the east country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the days of Abraham’s life were a hundred and seventy-five years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And decaying he died in a good old age, and having lived a long time, and being full of days: and was gathered to his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Isaac and Ismael his sons buried him in the double cave, which was situated in the field of Ephron the son of Seor the Hethite, over against Mambre,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which he had bought of the children of Heth: there was he buried, and Sara his wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after his death, God blessed Isaac his son, who dwelt by the well named Of the living and seeing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the generations of Ismael the son of Abraham, whom Agar the Egyptian, Sara’s servant, bore unto him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And these are the names of his children according to their calling and generations. The firstborn of Ismael was Nabajoth, then Cedar, and Adbeel, and Mabsam,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Masma, and Duma, and Massa,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hadar, and Thema, and Jethur, and Naphis, and Cedma.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the sons of Ismael: and these are their names by their castles and towns, twelve princes of their tribes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the years of Ismael’s life were a hundred and thirty-seven, and decaying he died, and was gathered unto his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he dwelt from Hevila as far as Sur, which looketh towards Egypt, to them that go towards the Assyrians. He died in the presence of all his brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These also are the generations of Isaac the son of Abraham: Abraham begot Isaac:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who when he was forty years old, took to wife Rebecca the daughter of Bathuel the Syrian of Mesopotamia, sister to Laban.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Isaac besought the Lord for his wife, because she was barren: and he heard him, and made Rebecca to conceive.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the children struggled in her womb, and she said: If it were to be so with me, what need was there to conceive? And she went to consult the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answering, said: Two nations are in thy womb, and two peoples shall be divided out of thy womb, and one people shall overcome the other, and the elder shall serve the younger.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when her time was come to be delivered, behold twins were found in her womb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that came forth first was red, and hairy like a skin: and his name was called Esau. Immediately the other coming forth, held his brother’s foot in his hand: and therefore he was called Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Isaac was threescore years old when the children were born unto him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were grown up, Esau became a skilful hunter, and a husbandman: but Jacob, a plain man, dwelt in tents.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Isaac loved Esau, because he ate of his hunting: and Rebecca loved Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jacob boiled pottage: to whom Esau, coming faint out of the field,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Said: Give me of this red pottage, for I am exceeding faint. For which reason his name was called Edom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jacob said to him: Sell me thy first birthright.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He answered: Lo I die, what will the first birthright avail me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jacob said: Swear therefore to me. Esau swore to him, and sold his first birthright.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And so taking bread and the pottage of lentils, he ate, and drank, and went on his way; making little account of having sold his first birthright.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when a famine came in the land, after that barrenness which had happened in the days of Abraham, Isaac went to Abimelech, king of the Palestines, to Gerara.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord appeared to him, and said: Go not down into Egypt, but stay in the land that I shall tell thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And sojourn in it, and I will be with thee, and will bless thee: for to thee and to thy seed I will give all these countries, to fulfil the oath which I swore to Abraham thy father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will multiply thy seed like the stars of heaven: and I will give to thy posterity all these countries: and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because Abraham obeyed my voice, and kept my precepts and commandments, and observed my ceremonies and laws.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Isaac abode in Gerara.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was asked by the men of that place, concerning his wife, he answered: She is my sister: for he was afraid to confess that she was his wife, thinking lest perhaps they would kill him because of her beauty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when very many days were passed, and he abode there, Abimelech, king of the Palestines, looking out through a window, saw him playing with Rebecca, his wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And calling for him, he said: It is evident she is thy wife: why didst thou feign her to be thy sister? He answered: I feared lest I should die for her sake.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Abimelech said: Why hast thou deceived us? Some man of the people might have lain with thy wife, and thou hadst brought upon us a great sin. And he commanded all the people, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that shall touch this man’s wife, shall surely be put to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Isaac sowed in that land, and he found that same year a hundredfold: and the Lord blessed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the man was enriched, and he went on prospering and increasing, till he became exceeding great.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he had possessions of sheep and of herds, and a very great family. Wherefore the Palestines envying him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Stopped up at that time all the wells, that the servants of his father, Abraham, had digged, filling them up with earth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Insomuch that Abimelech himself said to Isaac: Depart from us, for thou art become much mightier than we.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So he departed, and came to the torrent of Gerara, to dwell there:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he digged again other wells, which the servants of his father, Abraham, had digged, and which, after his death, the Philistines had of old stopped up: and he called them by the same names, by which his father before had called them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they digged in the torrent, and found living water:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But there also the herdsmen of Gerara strove against the herdsmen of Isaac, saying: It is our water. Wherefore he called the name of the well, on occasion of that which had happened, Calumny.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they digged also another; and for that they quarrelled likewise, and he called the name of it, Enmity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Going forward from thence, he digged another well, for which they contended not; therefore he called the name thereof, Latitude, saying: Now hath the Lord given us room, and made us to increase upon the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he went up from that place to Bersabee,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where the Lord appeared to him that same night, saying: I am the God of Abraham thy father, do not fear, for I am with thee: I will bless thee, and multiply thy seed for my servant Abraham’s sake.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he built there an altar: and called upon the name of the Lord, and pitched his tent; and commanded his servants to dig a well.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To which place when Abimelech, and Ochozath his friend, and Phicol chief captain of his soldiers, came from Gerara,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Isaac said to them: Why are ye come to me, a man whom you hate, and have thrust out from you?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they answered: We saw that the Lord is with thee, and therefore we said: Let there be an oath between us, and let us make a covenant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou do us no harm, as we on our part have touched nothing of thine, nor have done any thing to hurt thee; but with peace have sent thee away, increased with the blessing of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made them a feast, and after they had eaten and drunk:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arising in the morning, they swore one to another: and Isaac sent them away peaceably to their own home.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold, the same day the servants of Isaac came, telling him of a well which they had digged, and saying: We have found water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whereupon he called it Abundance: and the name of the city was called Bersabee, even to this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Esau being forty years old, married wives, Judith, the daughter of Beeri, the Hethite, and Basemath, the daughter of Elon, of the same place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they both offended the mind of Isaac and Rebecca.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim, and he could not see: and he called Esau, his elder son, and said to him: My son? And he answered: Here I am.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his father said to him, Thou seest that I am old, and know not the day of my death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take thy arms, thy quiver, and bow, and go abroad; and when thou hast taken something by hunting,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Make me a savoury meat thereof, as thou knowest I like, and bring it that I may eat: and my soul may bless thee, before I die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Rebecca had heard this, and he was gone into the field to fulfil his father’s commandment,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: She said to her son Jacob: I heard thy father talking with Esau, thy brother, and saying to him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bring me of thy hunting, and make me meats that I may eat, and bless thee in the sight of the Lord, before I die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now therefore, my son, follow my counsel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And go thy way to the flock, bring me two kids of the best, that I may make of them meat for thy father, such as he gladly eateth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which when thou hast brought in, and he hath eaten, he may bless thee before he die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
