text: And Jacob blessed the sons of Joseph, and said: God, in whose sight my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, God that feedeth me from my youth until this day:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The angel that delivereth me from all evils, bless these boys: and let my name be called upon them, and the names of my fathers Abraham and Isaac; and may they grow into a multitude upon the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Joseph seeing that his father had put his right hand upon the head of Ephraim, was much displeased: and taking his father’s hand, he tried to lift it from Ephraim’s head, and to remove it to the head of Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to his father: It should not be so, my father; for this is the firstborn, put thy right hand upon his head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he refusing, said: I know, my son, I know: and this also shall become a people, and shall be multiplied; but his younger brother shall be greater than he; and his seed shall grow into nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he blessed them at that time, saying: In thee shall Israel be blessed, and it shall be said: God do to thee as to Ephraim, and as to Manasses. And he set Ephraim before Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to Joseph, his son: Behold I die, and God will be with you, and will bring you back into the land of your fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I give thee a portion above thy brethren, which I took out of the hand of the Amorrhite with my sword and bow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jacob called his sons, and said to them: Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you the things that shall befall you in the last days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gather yourselves together, and hear, O ye sons of Jacob, hearken to Israel, your father:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ruben, my firstborn, thou art my strength, and the beginning of my sorrow; excelling in gifts, greater in command.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou art poured out as water, grow thou not; because thou wentest up to thy father’s bed, and didst defile his couch.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Simeon and Levi brethren: vessels of iniquity waging war.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let not my soul go into their counsel, nor my glory be in their assembly: because in their fury they slew a man, and in their self-will they undermined a wall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be their fury, because it was stubborn: and their wrath, because it was cruel: I will divide them in Jacob, and will scatter them in Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Juda, thee shall thy brethren praise: thy hand shall be on the necks of thy enemies; the sons of thy father shall bow down to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Juda is a lion’s whelp: to the prey, my son, thou art gone up: resting thou hast couched as a lion, and as a lioness, who shall rouse him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sceptre shall not be taken away from Juda, nor a ruler from his thigh, till he come that is to be sent, and he shall be the expectation of nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Tying his foal to the vineyard, and his ass, O my son, to the vine. He shall wash his robe in wine, and his garment in the blood of the grape.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His eyes are more beautiful than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Zabulon shall dwell on the seashore, and in the road of ships, reaching as far as Sidon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Issachar shall be a strong ass, lying down between the borders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saw rest that it was good: and the land that it was excellent: and he bowed his shoulder to carry, and became a servant under tribute.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Dan shall judge his people like another tribe in Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let Dan be a snake in the way, a serpent in the path, that biteth the horse’s heels, that his rider may fall backward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will look for thy salvation, O Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gad, being girded, shall fight before him: and he himself shall be girded backward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Aser, his bread shall be fat, and he shall yield dainties to kings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nephthali, a hart let loose, and giving words of beauty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Joseph is a growing son, a growing son and comely to behold: the daughters run to and fro upon the wall;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they that held darts, provoked him, and quarrelled with him, and envied him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His bow rested upon the strong, and the bands of his arms and his hands were loosed, by the hands of the mighty one of Jacob: thence he came forth a pastor, the stone of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The God of thy father shall be thy helper, and the Almighty shall bless thee with the blessings of heaven above, with the blessings of the deep that lieth beneath, with the blessings of the breasts and of the womb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The blessings of thy father are strengthened with the blessings of his fathers: until the desire of the everlasting hills should come: may they be upon the head of Joseph, and upon the crown of the Nazarite among his brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Benjamin a ravenous wolf, in the morning shall eat the prey, and in the evening shall divide the spoil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel: these things their father spoke to them, and he blessed every one with their proper blessings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he charged them, saying: I am now going to be gathered to my people: bury me with my fathers in the double cave, which is in the field of Ephron the Hethite,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Over against Mambre, in the land of Chanaan, which Abraham bought together with the field, of Ephron the Hethite, for a possession to bury in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There they buried him, and Sara his wife: there was Isaac buried with Rebecca, his wife: there also Lia doth lie buried.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had ended the commandments, wherewith he instructed his sons, he drew up his feet upon the bed, and died: and he was gathered to his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Joseph saw this, he fell upon his father’s face, weeping and kissing him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he commanded his servants, the physicians, to embalm his father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And while they were fulfilling his commands, there passed forty days: for this was the manner with bodies that were embalmed, and Egypt mourned for him seventy days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the time of the mourning being expired, Joseph spoke to the family of Pharao: If I have found favour in your sight, speak in the ears of Pharao:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For my father made me swear to him, saying: Behold I die; thou shalt bury me in my sepulchre which I have digged for myself in the land of Chanaan. So I will go up and bury my father, and return.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Pharao said to him: Go up and bury thy father according as he made thee swear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So he went up, and there went with him all the ancients of Pharao’s house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the house of Joseph with his brethren, except their children, and their flocks and herds, which they left in the land of Gessen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He had also in his train chariots and horsemen: and it was a great company.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is situated beyond the Jordan: where celebrating the exequies with a great and vehement lamentation, they spent full seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the inhabitants of Chanaan saw this, they said: This is a great mourning to the Egyptians. And therefore the name of that place was called, The mourning of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So the sons of Jacob did as he had commanded them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And carrying him into the land of Chanaan, they buried him in the double cave, which Abraham had bought together with the field for a possession of a burying place, of Ephron, the Hethite, over against Mambre.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Joseph returned into Egypt with his brethren, and all that were in his company, after he had buried his father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now he being dead, his brethren were afraid, and talked one with another: Lest perhaps he should remember the wrong he suffered, and requite us all the evil that we did to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they sent a message to him, saying: Thy father commanded us before he died,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That we should say thus much to thee from him: I beseech thee to forget the wickedness of thy brethren, and the sin and malice they practised against thee: we also pray thee, to forgive the servants of the God of thy father this wickedness. And when Joseph heard this, he wept.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his brethren came to him; and worshipping prostrate on the ground, they said: We are thy servants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answered them: Fear not: can we resist the will of God?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You thought evil against me: but God turned it into good, that he might exalt me, as at present you see, and might save many people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not: I will feed you and your children. And he comforted them, and spoke gently and mildly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he dwelt in Egypt with all his father’s house; and lived a hundred and ten years. And he saw the children of Ephraim to the third generation. The children also of Machir, the sons of Manasses, were born on Joseph’s knees.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After which he told his brethren: God will visit you after my death, and will make you go up out of this land, to the land which he swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made them swear to him, saying: God will visit you, carry my bones with you out of this place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he died, being a hundred and ten years old. And being embalmed, he was laid in a coffin in Egypt.
